channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Porzingis hits eight 3s, scores 34 as Bulls open 3-game road trip with blowout loss in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Kristaps Porzingis scored 26 of his season-high 34 points in the first half Wednesday night, and the Boston Celtics coasted to a 122-100 victory over the Bulls in the first game of Chicago's three-game road trip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2 teens from a Boston skating club, their mothers, and coaches among passengers on downed flight
item.description: Passengers aboard the American Airlines flight that collided with an Army helicopter and crashed into the Potomac River included teen figure skaters returning from the U.S. Figure Skating Championships and their Russian coaches.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Strength-sapping defense fuels No. 6 Houston's 13-game winning streak
item.description: MORGANTOWN, W.Va. (AP) — West Virginia’s Javon Small gestured and yelled for a teammate — any teammate — to get open closer to the basket. When that didn’t work, the Big 12's leading scorer forced up a 3-pointer just before the shot clock expired. Clank. Small was the latest star stifled by No.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: IOC presidential candidates pitch to Olympic voters in quirky closed-door event
item.description: Behind closed doors at the IOC seven candidates hoping to lead the Olympic body are making key pitches to an exclusive club of more than 100 voters.
item.dc:creator: Graham Dunbar, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 52 but Curry and Wiggins lead balanced Warriors past Thunder 116-109
item.description: Stephen Curry hit a long 3-pointer with 1:52 left on the way to 21 points, Andrew Wiggins scored 27 and the Golden State Warriors beat the Western Conference-leading Oklahoma City Thunder 116-109.
item.dc:creator: Janie Mccauley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Chiefs will seek another Super Bowl sweep in a title game rematch against the Eagles
item.description: Patrick Mahomes and the Kansas City Chiefs keep finding themselves on one side of a Super Bowl rematch.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dubow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Frustration boils over for Spurs' Wembanyama after shove by Zubac sends him flying
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Victor Wembanyama has had enough. The San Antonio star charged after Ivica Zubac after the Los Angeles center shoved him to the court during the Clippers' 128-116 victory over the Spurs on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: RAUL DOMINGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nikola Jokic says the surging New York Knicks are a 'top-5 candidate' to win the NBA title
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two years after he won an NBA title, Nikola Jokic views the surging New York Knicks as a “top-five candidate” to end their 52-year championship drought. The Knicks beat Jokic's Denver Nuggets 122-112 on Wednesday for their fifth straight victory.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Royals agree with RHP Carlos Estévez on 2-year, $22M deal, person familiar with deal tells AP
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — The Royals and reliever Carlos Estévez have agreed to a two-year, $22 million contract, giving Kansas City another potential closer alongside Lucas Erceg, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US Open tennis tournament is adding a 15th day by moving to a Sunday start in 2025
item.description: The U.S. Open is expanding to 15 days this year, shifting to a Sunday start for the first time in the Open era that began in 1968.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jazz drop fifth straight, losing at Memphis, 125-103
item.description: MEMPHIS (AP) - Jaren Jackson Jr. scored 28 points, Zach Edey added 19 points and four blocks and the Memphis Grizzlies won their sixth straight by beating the Utah Jazz 125-103 on Saturday night. Utah has now lost five in a row.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
